summary: AICON ("AICG content distribution platform", repo folder ai-moive-studio) is a Chinese-language full-stack AI content production workbench: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy(async) + Celery + Redis + PostgreSQL + MinIO backend, Vue 3 + Element Plus + Pinia + Konva frontend. It has four product surfaces: (1) Movie Studio â€” a 7-step pipeline that turns a novel chapter into a finished movie (characters â†’ scenes â†’ shots â†’ scene images â†’ keyframes â†’ 8-second transition videos â†’ FFmpeg final cut); (2) Picture Narration ("å›¾æ–‡è¯´") â€” sentence-level narrated slideshow videos (per-sentence image prompt â†’ image â†’ TTS audio â†’ Whisper-timed subtitles â†’ Ken Burns FFmpeg video â†’ concat); (3) Canvas â€” an infinite Konva canvas with text/image/video nodes, @-mention references, connections, generation history, plus a LangGraph ReAct "Canvas Assistant" agent that scaffolds a scriptâ†’charactersâ†’storyboardâ†’keyframeâ†’video node graph; (4) Distribution â€” Bilibili upload via the biliup CLI and JianYing (CapCut China) draft export. All AI calls route through user-supplied API keys (encrypted at rest) against an OpenAI-compatible relay (default https://api.aiconapi.me/v1), with Gemini image models and a "VectorEngine" Veo 3.1 video API as the generation backends.
architecture:
BACKEND (backend/src): layered FastAPI app. core/ (config via Pydantic Settings, async SQLAlchemy database, JWT security, logging); models/ (SQLAlchemy, BaseModel = UUID PK + created_at/updated_at timestamps, all UTC); api/v1/ routers (auth, users, files, projects, chapters, paragraphs, sentences, api-keys, prompt, image, audio, bgms, tasks, video-tasks, dashboard, bilibili, export, movie/* x5, canvas, canvas-assistant) + api/schemas Pydantic DTOs + api/websocket; services/ â€” the real business layer, one service per pipeline stage (MovieCharacterService, SceneService, StoryboardService, SceneImageService, VisualIdentityService for keyframes, TransitionService, MovieVideoService, plus PromptService/ImageService/AudioService/VideoSynthesisService for picture narration, CanvasService/CanvasGenerationService, BilibiliService, JianYingExportService, DashboardService, GenerationHistoryService); services/provider/ â€” pluggable AI providers behind BaseLLMProvider (completions / generate_image / generate_audio) created by ProviderFactory by string name (openai, deepseek, volcengine, siliconflow, custom, vectorengine); tasks/ â€” Celery tasks that are thin wrappers calling services via an async_task_decorator that supplies a per-task AsyncSession and runs coroutines on a worker event loop; utils/ â€” MinIO storage client (presigned URLs, object keys like uploads/{user}/...), ffmpeg_utils (concat fast/crossfade modes, zoompan Ken Burns builder, BGM mixing, speed change), Fernet-style API key encryption, encoding detection, file handlers for txt/md/docx/epub.

DATA FLOW (movie): API endpoint enqueues Celery task â†’ task instantiates service with fresh AsyncSession â†’ service loads API key, builds prompt from MoviePromptTemplates, calls provider with asyncio.Semaphore-bounded concurrency â†’ result image/video downloaded â†’ uploaded to MinIO â†’ row updated + MovieGenerationHistory record written â†’ frontend polls GET /api/v1/tasks/{celery_task_id} (status PENDING/PROGRESS/SUCCESS/FAILURE) every 1s via utils/taskPoller.js. Long-running video generation is asynchronous on the provider side: TransitionService stores provider task ids, and a Celery beat job (movie.sync_transition_video_status, every 30s) polls VectorEngine, downloads finished MP4s into MinIO and flips status. Canvas video generation instead polls inline from the API process when the client asks for status.

PERSISTENCE: PostgreSQL for everything relational; MinIO buckets (aicg-files, aicg-avatars) for all media â€” DB stores object keys, presigned URLs (24h for images, 6h for videos) are minted on serialization. Redis is the Celery broker/result backend. Celery config: JSON serialization, acks_late, prefetch 1, task_time_limit 600s/soft 480s, results expire 1h.

FRONTEND (frontend/src): Vue 3 SFC + Pinia stores (auth, projects, generations, settings) + services/*.js axios layer (api.js has 401/422 interceptors distinguishing login failure from token expiry) + composables that encapsulate each workflow (useMovieWorkflow composes useCharacterWorkflow/useSceneWorkflow/useShotWorkflow/useTransitionWorkflow; useCanvasEditor + useCanvasGeneration + useCanvasAssistant for canvas; useDirectorEngine for picture narration). Router: /dashboard, /projects/:id, /projects/:id/studio (ContentStudio), /projects/:id/director (DirectorEngine), /projects/:id/chapters/:chapterId/movie-studio (MovieStudio), /canvas and /canvas/:id, /generation (VideoTasks), /bgm-management, /bilibili-accounts, /publish, /api-keys, /settings. JWT auth guard; token validity 7 days (10080 min).
domainModel:
Project (owner_id, title, description, type: picture_narrative | ai_movie, file_name/size/type[txt,md,docx,epub]/path/hash, word_count/chapter_count/paragraph_count/sentence_count, status: uploadedâ†’parsingâ†’parsedâ†’generatingâ†’completed/failed/archived, processing_progress 0-100, error_message) 1â€”N Chapter (title, content TEXT, chapter_number, counts, status: pendingâ†’confirmedâ†’generating_promptsâ†’generated_promptsâ†’materials_preparedâ†’generating_videoâ†’completed/failed, is_confirmed+confirmed_at, video_url, video_duration) 1â€”N Paragraph (content, order_index, counts, action: keep/edit/delete/ignore, is_confirmed) 1â€”N Sentence (content, order_index, image_url, image_prompt, image_style, audio_url, audio_duration float, sentence_video_key + sentence_video_duration + needs_regeneration + last_video_generated_at for per-sentence video caching, status: pendingâ†’generated_promptsâ†’generated_imageâ†’generated_audioâ†’completed/failed).

MOVIE side: MovieCharacter (project_id, name â€” convention "ä¸­æ–‡å (EnglishName)", role_description, visual_traits TEXT for image prompts, dialogue_traits, era_background, occupation, key_visual_traits JSON list of 3-4 strings, generated_prompt = stored three-view prompt, avatar_url, reference_images JSON list â€” the consistency anchor). MovieScript (chapter_id, status pending/generating/completed/failed) 1â€”N MovieScene (order_index, scene TEXT dense cinematic description, characters JSON name list, scene_image_url, scene_image_prompt) 1â€”N MovieShot (order_index, shot TEXT first/last-frame-lockable description, dialogue, characters JSON, keyframe_url â€” exactly ONE keyframe per shot). MovieShotTransition (script_id, from_shot_id, to_shot_id, order_index, video_prompt TEXT, video_task_id from provider, video_url MinIO key, status pending/processing/completed/failed, error_message, api_key_id, user_id). MovieGenerationHistory â€” polymorphic history table (resource_type: scene_image|shot_keyframe|character_avatar|transition_video, resource_id, media_type image|video, result_url, prompt, model, api_key_id, is_selected flag; composite indexes on type+id+selected) enabling version browsing and rollback per asset.

VideoTask (user_id, project_id, chapter_id, task_type: picture_narration | movie_composition, api_key_id, background_id â†’ BGM, gen_setting JSON text â€” defaults {resolution 1440x1080 4:3, fps 30, libx264, aac 192k, zoom_speed, subtitle_style{font Arial, size 70, white, bottom}, bgm_volume 0.15, video_speed}, status pipeline: pendingâ†’validatingâ†’downloading_materialsâ†’generating_subtitlesâ†’synthesizing_videosâ†’concatenatingâ†’uploadingâ†’completed/failed, progress, current_sentence_index for resume, video_key/url/duration, error_message+error_sentence_id). BGM (file_key, name, status). APIKey (user_id, provider enum [openai, azure, google, baidu, alibaba, volcengine, custom, siliconflow], name, api_key encrypted, base_url, status active/inactive/expired, usage_count, last_used_at). User (timezone, language, preferences JSON, avatar). PublishTask + BilibiliAccount (cookie file per account; bvid/aid results).

CANVAS: CanvasDocument (user_id, title, description) 1â€”N CanvasItem (item_type text|image|video, title, position_x/y, width/height defaults 320x220, content_json â€” holds text, prompt, promptTokens, resolvedMentions, media object keys, workflow_kind/workflow_step markers, aspectRatio, options; generation_config_json â€” api_key_id + model; last_run_status idle/pending/processing/completed/failed, last_run_error, last_output_json, z_index) ; CanvasConnection (source_item_id, target_item_id, source_handle/target_handle 'left'|'right'); CanvasItemGeneration (item_id, document_id, user_id, generation_type, request_payload_json {prompt, prompt_tokens, resolved_mentions, api_key_id, model, options}, status, result_payload_json {result_image_object_key | result_video_object_key, provider_task_id, provider_response}, error_message) â€” doubles as generation history per node. Canvas uses a custom GUID TypeDecorator so it also runs on SQLite in tests.
aiIntegration:
PROVIDER LAYER: ProviderFactory.create(provider, api_key, base_url, max_concurrency) â†’ BaseLLMProvider with three pass-through methods: completions(model, messages, **kw), generate_image(prompt, model, **kw), generate_audio(input_text, voice, model). CustomProvider (the default; base_url https://api.aiconapi.me/v1) wraps AsyncOpenAI with an asyncio.Semaphore; if model name contains "gemini" it instead POSTs raw Gemini generateContent to {base}/v1beta/models/{model}:generateContent?key=..., payload {contents:[{role:user, parts:[{text:prompt}, ...inline_data images]}], generationConfig:{responseModalities:["IMAGE"], imageConfig:{aspectRatio:"16:9", imageSize:"1K"}}}; up to 5 reference_images are downloaded (or fetched from MinIO if key starts with "uploads/") and base64-inlined â€” THIS is the character-consistency mechanism. Gemini responses are wrapped into an OpenAI-shaped object exposing data[0].b64_json+mime. VectorEngineProvider (video, Veo 3.1): POST {base}/video/create {prompt, images:[data URLs of first+last keyframes], model:"veo_3_1-fast" (UI also offers veo3.1, veo3.1-components, veo3.1-fast, veo3.1-4k, veo3.1-components-4k), aspect_ratio:"16:9", enable_upsample:true, enhance_prompt:true} â†’ returns id; GET /videos/{id} for status; GET /videos/{id}/content for download URL. Retry policy: retry_with_backoff â€” exponential 1sâ†’20s cap with jitter, 5 attempts (image service retries only 429/RateLimit; movie one retries everything). Concurrency budgets: prompt LLM 20, image gen 20, TTS 5, shot extraction 3 scenes, keyframes 3 scenes parallel but sequential within a scene (relay), transition prompt gen 5, transition video submit 5, downloads 5. Model catalogs are hardcoded per provider in APIKeyService.get_models (custom: text gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview/gemini-3.1-pro-preview; image gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview/gemini-3-pro-image-preview; audio gpt-4o-mini-tts/tts-1; video the veo3.1 family) and fetched live from SiliconFlow's /v1/models.

PROMPT TEMPLATES (translated; originals in backend/src/services/movie_prompts.py, movie_character_service.py, keyframe_prompt_builder.py, prompt.py, assistant/workflow_prompts.py):

1) CHARACTER EXTRACTION (LLM, json_object mode, chapter text truncated to 5000 chars): "You are a veteran casting director. Analyze the following movie script fragment and extract all main characters. Output JSON {characters:[{name, era_background (e.g. 1940s WWII, Victorian Era, Cyberpunk 2077, Modern era), occupation, role_description (identity/background/personality), visual_traits (detailed visual description for AI image generation: age, hair color/style, clothing, facial features, build), key_visual_traits [3-4 core visual features], dialogue_traits (speech style: calm, rude, humorous, bookish, accent)}]}. Rules: a character must use exactly one consistent name; if a character has both Chinese and English names use the format 'ChineseName (EnglishName)'; never output both 'Aldric' and 'Aldric (Aldric)'; infer era if unclear (default Modern era); prefer explicitly described visual traits, otherwise infer plausibly from identity and era." Extraction is idempotent: names are normalized (parenthetical stripped) and matched against existing project characters, which get updated instead of duplicated.

2) CHARACTER THREE-VIEW SHEET (image prompt, English in source): "CINEMATIC LIVE-ACTION CHARACTER REFERENCE featuring THREE CONSISTENT PHOTOGRAPHED VIEWS of the SAME REAL HUMAN ACTOR portraying {name}: front view, 90-degree side view, and back view, captured during the same on-set studio photoshoot. Character details: Name/Era/Occupation/Key Visual Traits. Live-Action Photography Requirements: ONE real human actor; SAME actor, costume, makeup in all three views; natural skin texture with pores; real fabric/metal wear; identical hairstyle, proportions, posture; neutral production stance. Cinematography: high-end live-action film stills, ARRI/RED look, real studio lighting, photographed not rendered, neutral or black studio background. STRICT CONSTRAINTS: NO 3D render / CGI / Unreal Engine / game character style / digital sculpt look. Aspect ratio 16:9. Place the English name in the top-left corner, clearly visible." When generating the avatar, the service appends: "IMPORTANT: Include the text '{char.name}' in the top-left corner of the image, clearly visible and readable."

3) SCENE EXTRACTION (LLM, json_object): "You are an internationally award-winning screenwriter and director who converts novel chapters into structured cinematic Scene data. Split the chapter into Scenes and write extremely information-dense, filmable scene descriptions, tagging which characters appear. HARD RULES: you may NOT invent characters; you may ONLY use names from the provided character list and they must match EXACTLY (e.g. 'æ¢…éœ²å¸Œäºš (Melusia)' must be returned verbatim, never shortened); characters[] lists appearing characters without ranking; empty array if none; output ONLY JSON {scenes:[{order_index, scene, characters[]}]} with no markdown/explanations. SCENE WRITING SPEC: the scene field is not a summary but cinematic text that can be 'seen' â€” include environment & space (location, terrain, architecture, weather, light, time), sound elements (ambience, footsteps, metal, wind, rain), character actions (concrete physical movement, position changes, interactions), conflict & tension, key dialogue inline in quotes, emotion shown ONLY through action/voice/pauses â€” psychological monologue forbidden. SPLITTING RULES: one Scene = one definite time and space; split when location or time clearly changes; action-heavy passages may run longer; do NOT write shot/camera language; keep film pacing â€” never one Scene per chapter." Includes a worked example scene (rain-lashed city gate confrontation) demonstrating density.

4) SHOT (STORYBOARD) EXTRACTION (LLM per scene, json_object): "You are an award-winning director and storyboard artist who splits a movie scene into Shots that can each become a FIXED 8-SECOND video. CORE MODEL-ADAPTATION PRINCIPLE: each Shot = one fixed 8s video; the video model only uses the Shot's STARTING frame state and ENDING frame state and interpolates the middle; therefore every shot description must imply a clear start state and end state; all key action results and emotional beats MUST be visible in the final frame; never rely on mid-shot changes invisible at the end. HARD RULES: no new characters; exact names from the list; characters[] or []; output ONLY JSON {shots:[{order_index, shot, dialogue (empty string if none), characters[]}]}. SHOT WRITING SPEC: a description lockable by 'first frame + last frame': start state (pose, object positions, composition, lighting), continuously visible action completable in 8s, end state, framing & shot size (close-up/medium/wide/angle), light & color, environment & props, emotion only through visible action â€” no psychology, no abstract emotion words. DIALOGUE RULES: max 1-2 key lines per 8s shot, must accompany visible action, emotional turns must land on the end frame. SPLITTING BUDGET: simple scene 2-3 shots, dialogue scene 3-5, action scene 4-6; target 3-5 per scene; split only on major viewpoint change, space change, time jump, or a needed new end-frame beat; do NOT split single simple actions, continuous actions, per-line dialogue, or minor angle changes."

5) SCENE IMAGE / ESTABLISHING SHOT (image prompt, English in source, two variants): (a) from scene description; (b) preferred variant builds from all shot descriptions: "Create a cinematic establishing shot based on the visual elements described in the following shots. LIVE-ACTION PHOTOGRAPH, not CGI. Analyze the shots and extract the COMMON ENVIRONMENTAL ELEMENTS (location type, architectural details, lighting conditions, weather, color palette, spatial layout). Apply the Veo 3.1 formula [Cinematography]+[Environment Subject]+[Atmospheric Context]+[Style & Ambiance]. CRITICAL: UNINHABITED ENVIRONMENT â€” extract only the environment, remove ALL human elements; no people, silhouettes, shadows or reflections of people, mannequins or human-shaped objects. Match the shots' lighting/time/weather/palette so the establishing shot feels like the same scene. Shot on professional cinema camera (ARRI Alexa, RED, Sony Venice), cinematic color grading, HDR detail. FORBIDDEN: 3D/CGI aesthetics, game graphics, perfect geometric smoothness, unnatural light, oversaturated colors, perfectly clean surfaces."

6) KEYFRAME (image prompt assembled by KeyframePromptBuilder, English in source): CORE_STYLE block ("MUST be a LIVE-ACTION PHOTOGRAPH... real human actors, real locations, professional cinema cameras, natural lighting, photorealistic skin/fabric, film grain & DOF" plus CINEMATOGRAPHY FOR VIDEO ANIMATION: "composition should support camera movement; include depth layers for parallax; avoid extreme angles; frame with spatial context") + VISUAL CONTINUITY block â€” first shot of a scene: "This is the FIRST shot in this scene. Use the scene image as the primary visual reference for environment, lighting, atmosphere"; subsequent shots: "This shot CONTINUES from the previous shot. Previous shot description: {first 200 chars}. Keep consistent lighting, palette, atmosphere; smooth transition; characters keep consistent appearance and positioning; environment shows logical progression" + SHOT DESCRIPTION {shot.shot} + optional "Dialogue context: {first 100 chars}" + TECHNICAL_SPECS (35mm/digital cinema, film color grading, video-ready composition: depth layers, action potential, breathing room) + FORBIDDEN_ELEMENTS (no 3D/CGI/game/anime/synthetic); if the shot has NO characters a much stricter NO-PEOPLE forbidden list is swapped in (no humans, faces, bodies, silhouettes, shadows/reflections of people, crowds, mannequins). Ends "Remember: This is a REAL PHOTOGRAPH from a LIVE-ACTION FILM, not a digital creation." Reference images passed alongside: previous shot's keyframe (or scene image for shot #1) + avatar of every character present (relay strategy). Note: character visual_traits text exists in the builder but the active template intentionally relies on avatar reference images instead.

7) TRANSITION VIDEO PROMPT (LLM generates a Veo prompt; meta-prompt translated): "You are a film-grade video prompt expert mastering Google Veo 3.1. Generate ONLY the current shot's 8-second video content. IMPORTANT CONTEXT (not to be generated): the previous shot has already fully played â€” never repeat/replay/continue it; the video MUST start directly at the current shot's start state; previous-shot info is only for continuity understanding. [Previous shot â€” context only, DO NOT generate] {from_shot description+dialogue+characters}. [Current shot â€” what you generate] {to_shot ...}. HARD CONSTRAINTS: fixed 8 seconds; single continuous shot (no cuts/transitions); no details from the previous shot; no flashbacks or delayed entry; no new characters/props/scenes; all changes must be valid in the final frame. OUTPUT EXACTLY FIVE SECTIONS, no extra text: [Cinematography] camera type/movement (dolly, tracking, slow pan, push in, pull back, arc, handheld, steadicam), shot size, focal behavior (shallow DOF, deep focus, rack focus), composition; single continuous shot. [Subject] each character (names exactly as input) with â‰¤1-sentence start state and end state. [Action] the single 8s timeline: 0-2s establishing/start, 2-6s core action, 6-8s completion and frame settle; weave dialogue in naturally at the right moment; dialogue language rule: Chinese-named characters speak Chinese, English-named speak English, default Chinese; dialogue format: quotes + speaker and tone, e.g. Marcus Thorne says in a low rasp: \"Don't look at it, kid.\" [Context] location, light, time, weather, ambient sound. AUDIO RULES (critical): absolutely NO background music/BGM/soundtrack/score; only real diegetic sound with prefixes 'SFX:', 'Ambient noise:', 'Dialogue:'. [Style & Ambiance] live-action realism, cinematic, photorealistic; tones; mood through visible state only. Must end with the declaration: 'NO background music, NO BGM, NO soundtrack, only natural sound effects and ambient noise.' Includes a generation checklist. Output the full Chinese 8s video prompt only." The resulting prompt is sent to VectorEngine with the from/to keyframes as base64 data-URL images (first/last frame conditioning).

8) PICTURE NARRATION IMAGE-PROMPT SYSTEM PROMPT (PromptService): "You are a professional AI painting prompt expert (AI Director). Convert Chinese novel sentences into high-quality prompts. Rules: plain text only, no markdown or explanations; no prefixes like 'Here is the prompt:'; suggested structure Subject, Action, Background, Lighting, Style, Quality; express the sentence's visual elements, emotion and mood; if a sentence has no concrete picture (e.g. inner monologue), generate an atmosphere-matching symbolic image." + style suffix from presets: cinematic ("Cinematic lighting, 8k resolution, photorealistic, movie still, detailed texture, dramatic atmosphere"), anime ("Anime style, Makoto Shinkai style, vibrant colors, detailed background, high quality"), illustration, ink (Chinese ink painting). User message = the raw sentence. Custom system prompt overridable from UI.

9) SUBTITLE LLM CORRECTION (SubtitleService): system prompt: "You are a professional subtitle proofreader. I give you the original text and the Whisper-recognized subtitle timeline JSON; fix typos in each segment's text (and words[].word) by comparing with the original; NEVER delete/add/reorder words; keep word counts and order identical; keep JSON structure and all start/end times unchanged." Whisper (faster-whisper, model small, CPU float32) is primed with the original sentence as initial_prompt.

10) CANVAS ASSISTANT (LangGraph create_react_agent over a custom ChatModel that calls the user's LLM with response_format json_object; the model must answer {"kind":"tool_call","tool_name","args","message"} or {"kind":"final","message"}). System prompt (translated highlights): "You are Aicon Canvas Agent, an AI video-creation orchestration assistant living in an infinite canvas. Your primary goal is creating/splitting/deriving/optimizing/referencing/connecting nodes, not chatting. Only three node types exist: text, image, video. Return JSON only. Before each response reason about: which node types, reuse vs create, references, connections, one node or many. For multi-node tasks you MUST use batch tools â€” looping single create/update calls is forbidden. 'Write it to the canvas' means real canvas mutations happened. 'Split into 8 storyboards' means 8 nodes were created. Inspect before deriving from existing content. A failed tool call must not be retried verbatim â€” change args, change tool, or ask. Destructive actions require human confirmation (LangGraph interrupt with approve/reject). Canonical chains: idea/storyâ†’scriptâ†’storyboardâ†’imagesâ†’video; character designâ†’character imageâ†’shot imageâ†’video; reference imageâ†’promptâ†’video promptâ†’video. When workflow.target_stages is provided treat it as the main intent. For the main video chain use workflow_* tools, never fake placeholder nodes via canvas_create_item. If workflow.parameters already contains values treat them as confirmed; if workflow.missing_fields is non-empty never call workflow_prepare_script â€” ask for the missing fields first. workflow.prepare_from_script ONLY creates nodes and connections; three-view/keyframe/video generation must be manually triggered by the user unless explicitly requested. Legacy rigid rules retained: the script stage locks idea, script_type, style_id, language, duration_target, shot_duration_seconds; the character stage outputs stable names and three_view_prompt; the storyboard stage outputs storyboard_text, keyframe_prompt, video_prompt." Tools: workflow_get_status, workflow_prepare_script, workflow_prepare_from_script, workflow_generate_character_views/keyframes/videos, plus canvas inspection (inspect_graph, find_items, read_item_detail, read_neighbors) and execution tools (create_item(s) with auto-layout, update_item(s), delete_items [interrupt-gated], create/delete connections) and generation_submit. Sessions stream over SSE with an InMemorySaver checkpointer.

11) ASSISTANT WORKFLOW PROMPTS (workflow_prompts.py, translated): (a) script: "You are a screenwriter and pre-production coordinator. From the user's idea generate a complete script that can flow directly into character extraction, storyboarding, keyframe and video generation. Output script text only â€” no markdown headers, no explanations. Must contain explicit characters, scenes, shot-able actions, emotional progression" + fields (core idea, script type, visual style, dialogue mode default sparse, language, target duration, per-shot seconds, tone, constraints, confirmed creative spec: reference vibe, capture medium, lens language, visual effects, primary tones, saturation, scene traits, must-include elements). (b) characters: casting-director JSON extraction identical in spirit to the movie one but adds character_type (human/animal/creature/machine/vehicle/object/other) and three_view_prompt â€” "a complete Chinese image-generation prompt emphasizing standard three-view layout, character consistency, full front/side/back, embodying {style_id} style". (c) storyboard: single LLM call returns {scenes:[{order_index, scene, characters, shots:[{order_index, narrative, characters, storyboard_text, keyframe_prompt, video_prompt}]}]} with the same 'first/last frame defines an N-second shot' doctrine, all three prompt fields in full Chinese, style constraints carried through verbatim. prepare_workflow_from_script then materializes: one image node per character (content carries three_view_prompt as draft_prompt, workflow_kind=character_view) at x=420 column, one text node per shot (storyboard text + keyframe_prompt + video_prompt embedded, workflow_kind=storyboard) at x=840, connections scriptâ†’character, scriptâ†’storyboard, characterâ†’storyboard, and pre-injects character-image mention tokens into downstream keyframe prompts so keyframe image nodes inherit three-view references automatically.

12) DIALOGUE PERFORMANCE PROMPT (DialoguePromptEngine, docs/å¯¹è¯è¡¨æ¼”æç¤ºè¯.txt): "You are a top-tier acting coach. From the shot visuals and the character's dialogue design a precise visual performance prompt for AI video generation ensuring lip-sync, facial micro-expression and gaze match the dialogue. Inputs: character traits, dialogue, shot visual description, performance hint. Output: one English prompt â‰¤70 words describing facial micro-expressions, lip movement (lip-sync), eye behavior; natural and dramatically charged; output prompt only." Example included.

13) NINE-GRID STORYBOARD PLAN (docs/ä¹å®«æ ¼æ–¹æ¡ˆæç¤ºè¯ â€” documented but NOT wired into code): generate one 4K 3x3 contact-sheet image per scene (one API call instead of nine; claimed 40-55% cost cut, inherently consistent style), with prompts for scene extraction (+scene_type dialogue|action|establishing and estimated_duration), a 9-shot storyboard JSON (grid_position, camera_work, lighting, visual_connection_to_next, unified visual_continuity_theme), a 4K grid image prompt (numbered cells 1-9, strict 3x3 borders, live-action only), then programmatic 3x3 cropping to nine 1280x720 stills feeding image-to-video. Worth considering as a cost-optimization mode in a rebuild.

CANVAS GENERATION ORCHESTRATION: submitting a node generation creates a CanvasItemGeneration with normalized request (prompt rebuilt from promptTokens: text tokens concatenated, text-node mentions inlined as resolved content, image-node mentions stripped from text but collected into reference_image_object_keys/urls, capped at a REFERENCE_IMAGE_LIMIT), then enqueues canvas.generate_text/image/video Celery tasks. Text supports SSE streaming straight from the API process. Image: custom provider gets aspect_ratio + reference images (style reference image takes priority slot). Video: VectorEngine create_video with reference image data-URLs; result either returned inline or polled; finished media re-hosted in MinIO; result applied to the node's content_json, with history kept per generation and an 'apply_generation' endpoint to switch the active version.

featureInventory.howItWorks: ProjectCreator uploads txt/md/docx/epub to MinIO (file hash dedupe), creates Project (type picture_narrative or ai_movie chosen by card UI), Celery project.process_uploaded_file decodes encoding, RegexChapterDetector splits chapters by prioritized patterns (ç¬¬Nç«  > ç¬¬NèŠ‚/å›ž > '1.' > Chapter/Part/Section N > '1ã€' > bracketed > ç¬¬Nå· lowest), paragraphs split, sentences split, batch-inserted with statistics; status/progress tracked on the project row; failed projects retryable.

Three-pane resizable layout: ChapterNav list (create/edit/delete/confirm chapter), ParagraphStream with per-paragraph keep/edit/delete/ignore actions, SentenceInspector detail pane. Chapter must be 'confirmed' before generation; confirmation banner offers jump to Director Engine.

Per confirmed chapter, sentence table with filters (has prompt/image/audio). Dialogs: GeneratePrompts (pick API key, LLM model, style preset cinematic/anime/illustration/ink, optional custom system prompt) â†’ Celery generate.generate_prompts (20-way concurrent LLM, writes sentence.image_prompt); RegeneratePrompts for selected sentence ids; BatchGenerateImages â†’ generate.generate_images (20-way image gen from each sentence.image_prompt, MinIO upload, marks needs_regeneration); GenerateAudio â†’ generate.generate_audio (TTS, 5-way; SiliconFlow voice format 'model:voice'; duration probed via ffmpeg); per-sentence editing of prompts and single-asset regeneration. Frontend polls /tasks/{id} each second.

VideoTask(picture_narration) â†’ generate.synthesize_video routes by project.type â†’ VideoSynthesisService: validate chapter status=materials_prepared and every sentence has image+audio; per sentence (3-way concurrency, with per-sentence MinIO video CACHE keyed sentence_videos/{id}.mp4 reused unless needs_regeneration): download image+audio, faster-whisper word-timed transcription primed with the sentence text, optional LLM typo correction of the timeline JSON, build drawtext/subtitle filter, FFmpeg zoompan Ken Burns (scale+pad to 1440x1080, zoom 1.0â†’1.15, slow drift, fps 30) with audio; concat all sentence clips with crossfade (fade 0.5s); optional speed change; optional BGM loop mix at volume 0.15; upload to MinIO; chapter.video_url updated; resumable via current_sentence_index.

featureInventory.feature: Movie Studio step 0 â€” character extraction & casting
featureInventory.howItWorks: CharacterPanel: 'extract characters' â†’ Celery movie.extract_characters (LLM JSON, merges with existing by normalized name, stores generated three-view prompt); per-character card shows era/occupation/traits, editable three-view prompt, upload reference images, generate avatar (movie.generate_character_avatar â€” uses the stored/edited prompt + chosen reference image indices, appends top-left name watermark instruction) and batch avatar generation for all missing (parallel).

featureInventory.feature: Movie Studio step 1 â€” scene extraction
featureInventory.howItWorks: ScenePanel â†’ movie.extract_scenes: deletes any existing MovieScript for the chapter (cascade) and rebuilds; LLM with character-name lockdown produces dense filmable scene descriptions + character tags; progress callback surfaces percent via Celery PROGRESS meta.

featureInventory.feature: Movie Studio step 2 â€” shot/storyboard extraction
featureInventory.howItWorks: ShotPanel grouped by scene â†’ movie.extract_shots: wipes all existing shots AND scene images, then 3-way concurrent per-scene LLM calls produce 8-second-video-shaped shots (start/end frame doctrine, dialogue, characters); single-scene re-extraction (movie.extract_single_scene_shots) deletes and regenerates just that scene's shots.

featureInventory.feature: Movie Studio step 3 â€” scene establishing images
featureInventory.howItWorks: SceneImagePanel â†’ movie.generate_scene_images: for each scene lacking scene_image_url, worker with OWN db session builds the from-shots establishing prompt (uninhabited environment, Veo formula), 20-way semaphore, image â†’ MinIO, scene.scene_image_url + scene_image_prompt saved, history row written; single-scene endpoint accepts custom prompt override. Optional step â€” keyframes no longer hard-require scene images.

featureInventory.feature: Movie Studio step 4 â€” keyframe relay generation
featureInventory.howItWorks: KeyframePanel â†’ movie.generate_keyframes: shots grouped by scene; scenes processed 3 at a time each with an independent AsyncSession; INSIDE a scene shots run strictly sequentially: shot1 references scene image (if any) + avatars of its characters; shot N references shot N-1's keyframe + avatars; prompt built by KeyframePromptBuilder with previous-shot continuity block; commit after every shot so failures don't lose progress; per-shot single regeneration endpoint accepts a user-edited prompt (frontend pre-builds the professional prompt for editing). History rows enable version switch via is_selected.

featureInventory.feature: Movie Studio step 5 â€” transition (shot) videos
featureInventory.howItWorks: Two-phase. (a) movie.create_transitions: collect all shots WITH keyframes in order, pair adjacent shots within the same scene only (cross-scene pairs skipped), skip existing pairs, 5-way concurrent LLM generates the five-section Veo prompt per pair, rows saved status=pending; per-transition prompt regeneration task exists. (b) movie.generate_transition_videos: for each pending transition load both keyframes as base64 data URLs, VectorEngine create_video(prompt, images=[from,to], model e.g. veo_3_1-fast), store provider task id, status=processing â€” each worker in its own db session. (c) Celery beat every 30s movie.sync_transition_video_status: poll provider; completed â†’ download MP4 (300s timeout) â†’ MinIO â†’ status completed + history row; failed â†’ error_message; network timeouts left for next sweep. TransitionPanel shows the pair's keyframes, editable prompt, per-item regenerate, batch generate, and video preview.

featureInventory.feature: Movie Studio step 6 â€” material check & final composition
featureInventory.howItWorks: MaterialCheckDialog aggregates readiness (avatars/scene images/keyframes/transition videos with missing counts); 'compose' creates VideoTask(movie_composition) â†’ movie_composition.movie_compose_video â†’ MovieVideoService: fetch chapter transitions ordered, validate completeness, 5-way concurrent download, FFmpeg concat (mode='fast'; crossfade and a frame-trim dedupe mode documented â€” trim ~35 leading frames of every clip after the first to remove duplicated boundary keyframes â€” exist in ffmpeg_utils), optional BGM mix, upload, chapter.video_url + task completed.

WorkflowStepper across 7 steps; useMovieWorkflow auto-derives the current step from data state (no scriptâ†’0, no scenesâ†’1, no shotsâ†’2, missing keyframesâ†’4 (scene images skippable), no transitionsâ†’5, else 6) and gates each action (e.g. keyframes need all avatars; transitions need all keyframes).

Every avatar/scene-image/keyframe/transition generation writes MovieGenerationHistory (prompt, model, api_key, result_url, is_selected). GenerationHistoryPanel/Drawer lists versions with prompt preview; selecting one unselects others and patches the live URL on the owning row. Canvas has the same concept via CanvasItemGeneration + apply_generation.

vue-konva stage: connections layer (cubic bezier from left/right handles, control offset 60-120px) under items layer (group per node: rounded rect, optional media image/video-frame fill, title, preview text, two circular link handles). Wheel zoom about pointer, stage drag pan, Shift+drag marquee multi-select with batch delete confirmation, drag-end persists position, handle pointerdown starts a link-drag overlay that opens a 'create linked node' menu on empty drop. Text nodes render as HTML overlays (rich text) positioned via stage transform; status chips and animated 'generating' overlays float above nodes. Node sizing auto-fits media aspect ratio (canvasImageNodeLayout/canvasVideoNodeLayout). Opening a canvas loads a lightweight stage snapshot (content projected to previews) for big-canvas performance, full content fetched per selected item.

Selecting a node swaps in CanvasTextStudio/CanvasImageStudio/CanvasVideoStudio: title edit, API-key + model dropdowns (model catalog fetched per key per type; new image/video nodes auto-fill default API key + model), aspect-ratio options, a mention editor (CanvasPromptMentionEditor) where @-mentions reference upstream nodes â€” text mentions inline their content into the final prompt, image mentions become reference images (style-reference upload slot too), generate button, history drawer, upload-own-media override, delete. Video studio polls get_video_task_status until completed/failed.

Chat panel with SSE streaming timeline showing tool-call cards and confirmation cards (approve/reject for destructive ops via LangGraph interrupts). Default workflow mode ONLY creates nodes+connections (script â†’ character three-views â†’ storyboards with embedded keyframe/video prompts â†’ keyframe/video placeholder nodes); generation is user-triggered on canvas. Keyframe placeholder nodes are pre-wired with character three-view mention references for consistency.

APIKeys.vue + settings panel: CRUD keys per provider with base_url override (default custom https://api.aiconapi.me/v1, trailing slash warned against), encrypted storage, status toggle, usage_count tracking, per-key model list endpoint /api-keys/{id}/models?type=text|image|video|audio.

VideoTasks.vue lists VideoTask rows with status/progress, create dialogs (CreateVideoTaskDialog for narration with gen_setting form: resolution, fps, subtitle style, BGM picker + volume, speed; CreateMovieTaskDialog for composition), retry failed (resume from current_sentence_index), preview dialog with presigned URL.
BGMManagement.vue uploads audio to MinIO (BGM rows), used by both synthesis paths; bgm_volume from gen_setting, looped to video length via ffmpeg amix.

BilibiliAccounts.vue manages accounts; login via biliup CLI command surfaced to user (cookie file per account stored server-side); Publish.vue creates PublishTask â†’ Celery bilibili_task.upload_chapter_to_bilibili downloads the chapter video from MinIO, shells out to biliup with title/desc/tags/cover, parses bvid/aid from output, updates status.

export API builds a JianYing draft folder: downloads all sentence images/audio, generates draft_content.json with 1920x1080 canvas, image+audio+subtitle tracks with microsecond timings, zips it for the user to drop into JianYing drafts â€” an 'edit it yourself' escape hatch for narration projects (requires materials_prepared).
Aggregated per-user stats (projects by status, content counts, generation counts, task queue snapshot, naive cost estimate), recent projects and activity feed.
DialoguePromptEngine.design_performance_prompt(shot, character) â†’ â‰¤70-word English lip-sync/micro-expression prompt; exists as service+doc, only loosely integrated.

Documented alternative pipeline (docs only): one 4K 3x3 grid image per scene â†’ crop to nine 720p stills â†’ image-to-video each â†’ concat; prompts and cropping strategy specified, not implemented in code.
api/websocket.py exists for push updates but the frontend relies on 1s HTTP polling of Celery task state instead.
Near-identical AsyncOpenAI wrappers differing only in base_url; the 'custom' provider covers all OpenAI-compatible gateways.

uiPatterns:
1) MOVIE STUDIO: full-height three-zone layout â€” left ChapterSelector sidebar, main column with back-button header, a horizontal 7-step WorkflowStepper (Characters / Scenes / Shots / Scene Images / Keyframes / Transitions / Compose) that is freely clickable but auto-positions from data state, and a body that v-shows one panel per step. Every panel follows the same grammar: an action bar (API-key select + model select + batch button with loading state, gated by canXxx computed), a card grid of assets (character cards with avatar + traits; scene cards with scene text + establishing image; shots grouped under scene headers showing keyframe thumb + shot text + dialogue chip; transition cards showing from/to keyframe pair + editable video prompt + status tag), and per-card single-item regenerate buttons that open a PromptDialog pre-filled with the builder-generated prompt for manual tweaking before submit. Long operations show progress from Celery PROGRESS meta. Final step is a checklist card (el-descriptions with green/red tags and missing counts) before the compose button appears.

2) CONTENT STUDIO: header toolbar (back, collapse toggles, project title, confirm-chapter, save), stats strip (chapters/paragraphs/words/modified count), three resizable panes with drag handles and slide transitions: ChapterNav | ParagraphStream (paragraph cards with keep/edit/delete/ignore) | SentenceInspector (maximizable). Confirming a chapter raises a success banner deep-linking to Director Engine.

3) DIRECTOR ENGINE: chapter dropdown of confirmed chapters; sentence table with tri-state filters (has prompt/image/audio); multi-select checkboxes feeding batch dialogs (GeneratePromptsDialog with style preset radio + custom system prompt textarea, BatchGenerateImagesDialog with model select, GenerateAudioDialog with voice select); each row expandable to edit image_prompt inline and regenerate one asset; thumbnails + audio players inline.

4) CANVAS EDITOR: full-bleed Konva stage; CanvasWorkbenchLayout overlays a top-left back/title bar, a zoom indicator ("87% view"), a link-mode hint, and a node-launcher (create text/image/video) shown only when nothing is selected; selecting a node opens a floating studio panel anchored beside the node (selectedItemStyle computed from stage transform) rather than a fixed sidebar â€” the node itself stops rendering on stage while its overlay editor is active (editing-item-id). Link creation = drag from a node's circular handle; dropping on empty canvas opens CanvasLinkCreateMenu to create-and-connect a typed node; dropping on a node creates the connection. Connection selection shows a floating delete pill at the curve midpoint. Shift+drag marquee â†’ multi-select â†’ batch delete with ElMessageBox confirm (also cleans dangling connections). Generation states render as animated overlay cards on the node plus colored status chips. History drawer slides from the right listing prior generations with thumbnails and an 'apply' action. The mention editor renders prompt as token chips: plain text + @node chips with resolved/pending status colors; image chips display thumbnail; the same tokens drive both prompt text assembly and reference-image collection. Assistant panel: timeline of user/assistant messages, collapsible tool-call summary rows (tool name, level icon, summary), confirmation cards with approve/reject buttons, streaming status indicator, composer with API-key/model pickers.

5) GLOBAL: MainLayout with AppSidebar navigation (Dashboard, Projects, Canvas, Generation tasks, BGM, Bilibili accounts, Publish, API Keys, Settings); Chinese-language UI throughout; ElMessage toasts for every async outcome; project cards with type badge (ai_movie vs picture_narrative illustrated cards at creation); date display localized to user timezone preference; 1s polling with attempt caps (default 60 attempts) and progress callbacks for percent text.
pitfalls: 1) ORM objects shared across asyncio tasks repeatedly caused DetachedInstanceError / 'Session is already flushing' â€” half the services hand-roll workarounds (extracting plain dicts before gather, opening a brand-new AsyncSession per worker/scene, commit-per-shot). A rebuild should make generation workers session-free by design (pass IDs + plain DTOs, single writer). 2) No real job orchestration: Celery tasks have max_retries=0, no idempotency keys, no dedupe â€” double-clicking batch buttons double-submits; the only recurring job is a global 30s beat poll over ALL processing transitions (full table scan, one-by-one provider polls, sequential). Prefer per-task scheduled polling or webhooks, with retry/backoff in the job layer. 3) Frontend learns Celery state by polling /tasks/{id} every second with a 60-attempt cap â€” long generations silently 'time out' in the UI while still running server-side; the WebSocket module exists but is unused. 4) Destructive regeneration semantics: extract_scenes deletes the whole script cascade; batch extract_shots wipes all shots AND scene images; there is no undo and history rows survive pointing at deleted resources. Make regeneration non-destructive/versioned. 5) Prompt templates are giant Python string constants scattered across movie_prompts.py, movie_character_service.py, keyframe_prompt_builder.py, assistant/workflow_prompts.py, and docs/*.txt that have drifted from code (docs versions differ); centralize and version prompts as data. 6) Provider quirks leak everywhere: 'gemini' substring sniffing chooses a different code path; Gemini's base64 wrapping is hand-rolled; VectorEngine returns 'id' vs 'task_id' and status sometimes nested in 'detail' â€” wrap providers behind a normalized adapter with typed results. 7) Inconsistent model-name conventions (veo_3_1-fast vs veo3.1-components) hardcoded in BOTH backend lists and frontend components; one model catalog source of truth needed. 8) Character extraction truncates input to 5000 chars silently â€” long chapters lose characters. 9) retry_with_backoff in services/image.py retries only rate limits but the movie version (imported from the same module name) retries ALL exceptions including permanent 4xx, wasting paid calls. 10) Security/code hygiene: __main__ test blocks with real-looking UUIDs/keys in service files, print() debugging, commented-out dead code, double API-key loading, Chinese/English mixed naming, monolithic 1800-line canvas.py mixing CRUD + generation + URL signing. 11) Transition videos only pair shots WITHIN a scene, so scene boundaries have no transition and the final concat relies on hard cuts (fast mode) â€” the dedup-trim crossfade path exists but is disabled; decide the boundary strategy explicitly. 12) Costs: keyframe relay is strictly sequential per scene (correct for consistency but slow); the documented nine-grid approach was never built â€” consider it as an optional fast/cheap mode. 13) gen_setting stored as a JSON string in a TEXT column rather than JSONB; canvas content_json mixes camelCase (promptTokens) and snake_case keys. 14) JWT note: CLAUDE.md says 30-min tokens but README changelog says 7 days â€” config drift between docs and code is pervasive; trust code.
